sourceMovie.title: The Babadook
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: The Babadook (2014) stuck because it commits to its drama premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Jennifer Kent's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The drama and horror blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Host
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Babadook clicked for you, The Host (2006) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and horror, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Babadook?
faq.answer: Try The Host, American Psycho, The Conjuring 2—each shares DNA with The Babadook while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Babadook?
faq.answer: The Babadook (2014) is best described as Drama, Horror. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Babadook worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.5/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
